title: cannot assemble cgroup path with base "/libpod_parent" - must end in .slice: invalid argument
body:
When I installed Fedora 32, I couldn't use Docker. Later, I followed some instructions to allow docker to run. Later, I moved to podman and disabled Docker again (presumably by changing systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=0). Now, whenever I start podman for the first time, I see this error, although it does the job (e.g. building the image):  

> ERRO[0000] Error creating CGroup for pod 29aabe91bd2d1eaf3805b00df904f40177357f75e0c499508a5086e873e4f8ea: cannot assemble cgroup path with base "/libpod_parent" - must end in .slice: invalid argument 

Just curious what this means and if I need to do anything.

comments.body:
Looks like a container created with cgroupfs, and now Podman is using the
Systemd driver and can't make sense of the cgroup parent the container was
made with. Best solution is probably to force cgroupfs in containers.conf
for now.

We should probably not hardcode cgroup parent at creation time (unless the
user sets it), too - can lead to things like this.

On Sun, Aug 2, 2020, 08:14 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon> PTAL
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7186#issuecomment-667666639>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCGHUTXL2T5OESQJX6DR6VKCDANCNFSM4PSP7ZNA>
> .
>

@rhatdan I tried `podman system reset` and executed build. It showed `sd-bus call: Permission denied`.  I rebooted the system and built and run the image without any problem.  No more errors of any kind! Why?

The sd-bus usually means that the containers can not communicate with the systemd dbus server.  I think this means your login account was not setup correctly or not initialized correctly.

We see this when people login as root and `su user`.  But if you login normaly via ssh or graphical login, systemd should setup the user session correctly.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no futher comments, I guess we have explained it.  Reopen if more discussion is necessary.
I confirm: The error message was gone for me as well after `podman system reset`.
